title: Earthquake
description: No warning before an earthquake — prepare before, not during.

items.text: Secure water heater to wall studs with strapping — fall hazard and fire risk
Secure bookcases, cabinets, and tall furniture to wall studs with L-brackets
Store minimum 14-day water supply (1 gal/person/day) in a low, stable location
Store 14-day supply of non-perishable food requiring no cooking
Learn to shut off gas at the meter — know the location and have a wrench
Keep a crescent wrench attached to your gas meter for emergency shutoff
Flashlight in every bedroom — power will fail, debris will be in the dark
Shoes stored under every bed — broken glass causes serious post-quake injuries
Drop-Cover-Hold On practiced with all household members
Know your tsunami evacuation zone if in a coastal area — shaking IS the warning
Emergency go-bag packed and accessible from outside building
Fire extinguisher in kitchen and garage — earthquake fires are common from gas leaks
Home insurance reviewed — standard policies often exclude earthquake damage
Prescription medications — 90-day supply if on ongoing medications
Battery banks and solar panel for device charging
Out-of-area emergency contact designated and written on paper
Important documents in waterproof container ready to take immediately
